bag of cards is dealt to the author in a game of poker where uncertainty plays a major role. bag of probability calculations involves determining the chance of receiving at least one heart in the next five cards. bag of combinatorial functions is used to express this probability. bag of total cards remaining is fifty minus two which is forty eight. bag of favorable outcomes is calculated by subtracting the number of hands with no hearts from the total number of possible five card hands. bag of total possible five card hands is combination of forty eight choose five. bag of hands with no hearts is combination of forty choose five. bag of probability is one minus combination of forty choose five divided by combination of forty eight choose five. bag of simplification leads to a closed form expression that represents the chance of getting at least one heart in the next five cards.

bag of decision theory is applied when evaluating the expected value of different strategies. bag of strategy A involves a 0.4 chance of winning one thousand dollars and a 0.6 chance of losing five hundred dollars. bag of expected value for strategy A is calculated by multiplying the probabilities with their respective outcomes and adding them together. bag of strategy B involves a 0.6 chance of winning eight hundred dollars and a 0.4 chance of losing two hundred dollars. bag of expected value for strategy B is similarly calculated by multiplying the probabilities with their outcomes and summing them. bag of comparison between the two expected values reveals that strategy A has a higher expected value than strategy B. bag of conclusion is that the author should choose strategy A for the highest expected value.
